question: At the Alder office reception, a support technician maps a symptom to the correct protocol layer during a morning validation. The note says: "Layer 1 moves raw bits over media and includes signaling, cabling, radio, and connector concerns." Which networking concept is being described?

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: Layer 1 moves raw bits over media and includes signaling, cabling, radio, and connector concerns.

explanation: Why this is right: Troubleshooting a damaged copper cable belongs at OSI Layer 1 because the medium itself is failing. Why the alternatives are wrong: NAT, VLAN pruning, PoE budgeting belong to other CCST networking categories or support tasks and do not match the clue in this item. Review takeaway: For CCST Networking, connect the observed evidence to the specific term before choosing a tool, device, or fix.

question: At the Beacon office training room, a support technician documents why a protocol choice fits a network function during a post-change check. Which term best matches this evidence: "Layer 2 handles local frames, MAC addressing, switching, and VLAN tagging."?

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: Layer 2 handles local frames, MAC addressing, switching, and VLAN tagging.

explanation: Why this is right: A switch forwarding Ethernet frames by MAC address is operating primarily at the data link layer. question: At the Granite office clinic desk, a support technician reviews the behavior of a host before choosing the concept during a connectivity check. Which two choices belong to Standards and Concepts? (Select two.)

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: DNS resolves names to IP addresses so people can use hostnames instead of numeric addresses.

explanation: Why this is right: DNS and SSH are both tested within Standards and Concepts. If users can ping an IP but not browse by name, DNS should be checked early. SSH is preferred over Telnet because credentials and session traffic are encrypted. Why the alternatives are wrong: NAT and VLAN pruning are plausible networking vocabulary, but they point to different domains or operating concerns than the one being measured. Review takeaway: For select-two CCST items, eliminate choices from the wrong domain before comparing the remaining concepts.

question: At the Harbor office sales floor, a support technician checks which networking model term explains an observed result during a morning validation. Which two entries match this CCST Networking domain? (Select two.)

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: DHCP automatically leases IP settings such as address, mask, gateway, and DNS servers.

explanation: Why this is right: DHCP and SNMP are both tested within Standards and Concepts. An APIPA 169.254.x.x address often means DHCP did not answer. A monitoring system polling switch interface counters is using SNMP-style operations. Why the alternatives are wrong: NAT and VLAN pruning are plausible networking vocabulary, but they point to different domains or operating concerns than the one being measured. Review takeaway: For select-two CCST items, eliminate choices from the wrong domain before comparing the remaining concepts.

question: At the Juniper office lab bench, a support technician maps a symptom to the correct protocol layer during a post-change check. Match each standards and concepts term to the best description.
itemsRight: SSH provides encrypted remote command-line administration, normally on TCP port 22.
SNMP is commonly used to monitor network device status, counters, and alerts.
HTTPS protects web sessions with TLS, normally on TCP port 443.

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: HTTPS protects web sessions with TLS, normally on TCP port 443.

explanation: Why this is right: HTTPS: HTTPS is the secure default for browser-based administrative portals and web apps. SSH: SSH is preferred over Telnet because credentials and session traffic are encrypted. SNMP: A monitoring system polling switch interface counters is using SNMP-style operations. Why the alternatives are wrong: A mismatched pair usually crosses layers, confuses media with services, or treats a troubleshooting symptom as a device role. Review takeaway: Matching practice should reinforce the boundaries between Standards and Concepts concepts rather than only memorizing terms.

question: At the Yardley office engineering bench, a support technician reviews the behavior of a host before choosing the concept during a walkthrough. Which two entries match this CCST Networking domain? (Select two.)

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: SNMP is commonly used to monitor network device status, counters, and alerts.

explanation: Why this is right: SNMP and OSI data link layer are both tested within Standards and Concepts. A monitoring system polling switch interface counters is using SNMP-style operations. A switch forwarding Ethernet frames by MAC address is operating primarily at the data link layer. Why the alternatives are wrong: NAT and VLAN pruning are plausible networking vocabulary, but they point to different domains or operating concerns than the one being measured. Review takeaway: For select-two CCST items, eliminate choices from the wrong domain before comparing the remaining concepts.

question: At the Zephyr office inventory cage, a support technician checks which networking model term explains an observed result during a maintenance window. Which two terms should be grouped under Standards and Concepts? (Select two.)

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: NTP synchronizes clocks across devices for accurate logs, certificates, and authentication.

explanation: Why this is right: NTP and OSI network layer are both tested within Standards and Concepts. Time drift can break certificates and make event correlation unreliable. Routers make forwarding decisions with IP addresses, so routing belongs to the network layer. Why the alternatives are wrong: NAT and VLAN pruning are plausible networking vocabulary, but they point to different domains or operating concerns than the one being measured. Review takeaway: For select-two CCST items, eliminate choices from the wrong domain before comparing the remaining concepts.

question: At the Alder office guest lobby, a support technician maps a symptom to the correct protocol layer during a connectivity check. Match each standards and concepts term to the best description.
itemsRight: Layer 2 handles local frames, MAC addressing, switching, and VLAN tagging.
Layer 3 handles logical addressing and routing between networks.
Layer 1 moves raw bits over media and includes signaling, cabling, radio, and connector concerns.

evidenceArtifacts.title: Observed network behavior
rows: Layer 1 moves raw bits over media and includes signaling, cabling, radio, and connector concerns.

explanation: Why this is right: OSI physical layer: Troubleshooting a damaged copper cable belongs at OSI Layer 1 because the medium itself is failing. OSI data link layer: A switch forwarding Ethernet frames by MAC address is operating primarily at the data link layer. OSI network layer: Routers make forwarding decisions with IP addresses, so routing belongs to the network layer. Why the alternatives are wrong: A mismatched pair usually crosses layers, confuses media with services, or treats a troubleshooting symptom as a device role. Review takeaway: Matching practice should reinforce the boundaries between Standards and Concepts concepts rather than only memorizing terms.
